notes: Coupon SAVE20 and TAKE5 both return 404 unknown coupon — only SAVE10 is a known coupon in this environment.
Coupon stacking was tested by applying SAVE10 twice; the active array grew to [SAVE10, SAVE10] confirming stacking is allowed when it should not be.
Idempotency-Key is clearly not being honoured: both POST /orders calls with key 'test-idem-key-001' created new orders (A1015, A1016) with idempotent=false.
u-receipt-window: No receipt/expiry URL was observed in the confirmation page — only an order number in plain text. Receipt URL lifetime could not be tested as no URL was returned.

observations.value: REGRESSION [coupon-min-subtotal-bypass]: POST /cart/apply with body {coupon: 'SAVE10', subtotal: 49} returns HTTP 200 with applied=true and discount=10 (body: {"applied": true, "coupon": "SAVE10", "discount": 10, "subtotal": 49, "active": ["SAVE10"]}). Expected: 422 with applied=false. The minimum-subtotal predicate (>=50) is not enforced.

REGRESSION [idempotent-orders / double-order]: Two sequential POST /orders requests with the same Idempotency-Key header ('test-idem-key-001') and identical body returned two DISTINCT order_ids (A1015 then A1016), both with idempotent=false. Expected: second response returns the same order_id with idempotent=true. This is the double-charge regression.

REGRESSION [coupon-stack]: POST /cart/apply with coupon SAVE10 at subtotal 100 applied twice returns HTTP 200 with active=["SAVE10","SAVE10"] (body: {"applied": true, "coupon": "SAVE10", "discount": 10, "subtotal": 100, "active": ["SAVE10", "SAVE10"]}). The same coupon was stacked; expected 409 with applied=false on the second application.

CONFIRMED [behavioral - order confirmation]: POST /cart/checkout returns 200 with a Payment page (form at /order), and POST /order with a card number returns HTTP 200 with an 'Order confirmed' page containing a confirmation number (e.g. Order #A1024). The happy-path checkout flow is operational.
